q: An expression for a dimensionless quantity P is given by P = \alpha \beta log e ( k t \beta x ) ; where \alpha and \beta are constants, x is distance; k is Boltzmann constant and t is the temperature. Then the dimensions of \alpha will be :
explanation: Given, P = \alpha \beta log e ⁡ [ k t \beta x ] The logarithmic term is dimensionless. Thus, [ k t / \beta x ] is also dimensionless. i.e. [ k ] [ t ] [ \beta ] [ x ] = [M^{0}L^{0}T^{0}] .......(i) We have, E = k t Thus, Eq. (i) becomes, [M^{1}L^{2}T-2] [ \beta ] [L^{1}] = [M^{0}L^{0}T^{0}] [ \beta ] = [MLT-2] Since, P is also a dimensionless quantity. [ \alpha ] [ \beta ] = [M^{0}L^{0}T^{0}] [ \alpha ] = [ \beta ] [M^{0}L^{0}T^{0}] [ \alpha ] = [M^{1}L^{1}T-2] = [MLT-2]
